mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling higher corporate earnings and leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is consistently below 0.02 over any rolling 36-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 8.0%, it indicates significant labor market weakness and economic distress, reducing aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, thus driving negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 8.2% in the latest release, triggering the condition.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8.0% over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) combined with accelerating inflation (3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5%) creates a 'behind the curve' stagflationary signal, compressing real earnings and increasing risk premia, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Policy rates are near zero post-2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a negative Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) serve as a powerful leading indicator of renewed consumer confidence and durable goods demand, boosting expectations for broad economic growth and driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Economy is in a prolonged recovery phase post-2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in HOUST and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during the 2010-2015 period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong job creation, measured by a 3-month average change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 150,000, indicates a robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenues and positive market sentiment.
failure_condition: The strategy of buying the S&P 500 when the trigger is active underperforms a buy-and-hold strategy over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (quarter-over-quarter annualized GDPC1) is positive but retail sales growth (month-over-month RSAFS) is negative, it signals a divergence where broad economic output is not supported by consumer spending, predicting a near-term drop in equity prices.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct directional predictions) of the signal is less than 55% over a 100-trade sample.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) is a direct measure of consumer spending strength. This strength translates directly into higher expected corporate revenues, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 5-day returns to monthly RSAFS surprises is not significantly positive over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) regime (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), any unexpected rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) heightens fears of a premature policy tightening, increasing the equity risk premium and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.16, firmly in the ZIRP regime.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL changes and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.
